rationale: Y identifies the seeding-reproducibility tradeoff under full cudagraphs (mirroring gcanlin's central inline concern about mtp seed failing at decode batch>1) and questions the NPU stage-1 PIECEWISE scoping (echoing Wallbreazzz's cherry-pick crash report), while X's four 'major' findings are all a repeated 'add a test for this path' template that, though grounded in real test-coverage gaps, doesn't overlap with what actual human reviewers debated. Both independently flag the same stale/contradictory YAML comments and the duplicate omni_pooler_payload_include_hidden assignment, so actionability is comparable, but Y's grounding in the PR's real review threads gives it clearly better recall and precision.
